page: Microsoft Azure M416s_9_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the MSHighMemoryv3 family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by Intel processors, delivering 416 dedicated vCPUs. The server is configured with 8,568.0 GB of system memory, which equates to approximately 20.6 GB of RAM per vCPU. It operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

This instance does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met using external volumes. It is Premium Storage capable, allowing the attachment of high-performance remote disk options to handle intensive input/output operations. There are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with this instance, and no graphics processing units are included in this configuration.

Given the massive memory footprint of over 8.5 TB and high vCPU count, this server represents a specialized hardware class designed for resource-dense workloads. The qualitative cost profile reflects its positioning as an enterprise-grade platform where massive memory capacity is prioritized over local storage. It is engineered for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise applications such as SAP HANA, massive data warehousing, and complex transactional database workloads that require substantial RAM to keep entire datasets in memory.
description: Microsoft Azure M416s_9_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the MSHighMemoryv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Powered by Intel x86_64 processors, it provides 416 dedicated vCPUs and an extensive memory capacity of 8,568.0 GB, yielding approximately 20.6 GB of RAM per core. This server does not feature local storage but is Premium Storage capable, enabling high-performance remote storage attachments. It contains no GPUs and offers zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Given its massive memory-to-vCPU ratio, this instance is designed for enterprise-scale, memory-intensive workloads. It is highly suited for running large-scale in-memory databases, data warehousing, and heavy transactional enterprise applications that demand substantial memory capacity to process massive datasets efficiently without relying on local disk storage.
